disclaimer.title: Important disclaimer
disclaimer.body1: The information and results produced by this website are for informational purposes only. They are based on the formulas and assumptions described in this tool and are not medical, legal, or professional advice. We make no warranties, express or implied, about accuracy or fitness for a particular purpose.
disclaimer.body2: If the underlying equations or their application are incomplete, outdated, or incorrect, the outputs shown here will also be wrong. Do not rely on this site as a substitute for qualified professional judgment, clinical testing, or legal review. We are not liable for any decisions, losses, or other consequences arising from use of this calculator. By continuing, you acknowledge and accept these limitations.
header.title: Visual Acuity Calculator
about.title: About This Tool
about.purpose: This calculator determines equivalent Snellen visual acuity from an object's physical size and its distance from the viewer. It also categorizes the viewing distance (Near, Intermediate, Far) and reports the expected accommodative demand.
about.visualAngleIntro: Visual Angle: Uses the exact geometric split-triangle formula to determine the subtended angle in arc-minutes:
about.equivalentAcuityIntro: Equivalent Acuity: Derives the Snellen denominator based on the standard 5 arc-minute letter:
about.accommodativeIntro: Accommodative Demand: Calculated in Diopters based on viewing distance:
about.alternateFormats: Alternate acuity (N = 20, D = Snellen denominator): MAR = D / N; LogMAR = log10(MAR); decimal = N / D; metric 6/X uses Dmetric = D × (6/20), shown as 6/round(Dmetric); M-units at the entered distance: M = distance_m × (D / N).
validation.sizeError: Please enter a valid object size greater than 0.
validation.distanceError: Please enter a valid viewing distance greater than 0.
announce.formError: Error in form submission. Please correct the invalid fields.
results.title: Equivalent Visual Acuity Report
results.marAria: Minimum angle of resolution in minutes of arc
results.munitsTitle: Near print size per ISO; uses your entered viewing distance in meters
announce.calcCompleteIntro: Calculation complete. For an object size of {sizeVal} {sizeUnitLabel} at a distance of {distVal} {distUnitLabel}, the equivalent visual acuity is 20 over {d}.
announce.altBlock: MAR is {mar} minutes of arc, LogMAR is {logmar}, decimal acuity is {decimal}, metric acuity is {metric}, and M-units at this distance is {munits} M.
